# Read the provided file
path = "/mnt/data/file-JEXHUqE6y2qsouCVE6wxDV-text_to_analyse.txt"
with open(path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    text = f.read()
len(text), text[:2000]

import re, json, textwrap, sys, math
text = open("/mnt/data/file-JEXHUqE6y2qsouCVE6wxDV-text_to_analyse.txt", "r", encoding="utf-8").read()
# find substrings in double quotes
spans = [(m.start(), m.end(), m.group(0)) for m in re.finditer(r'"[^"]*"', text)]
len(spans), spans[:3]

for i,(s,e,content) in enumerate(spans):
    print(i, s, e, content)

# Prepare the spans entries excluding quotes
entries = []
for i,(s,e,content) in enumerate(spans):
    start = s+1
    end = e-1
    span_text = text[start:end]
    # Determine citation type
    citation_type = "Hadeeth" if i == 1 else "Aya"
    entries.append({
        "span_start": start,
        "span_end": end,
        "span_text": span_text,
        "citation_type": citation_type
    })
# Verify substrings match exactly with indices in original
ok = all(text[d["span_start"]:d["span_end"]] == d["span_text"] for d in entries)
ok, len(entries)

# sanity check - surrounding characters
for i,d in enumerate(entries):
    s = d["span_start"]; e = d["span_end"]
    prev = text[s-1] if s-1 >= 0 else None
    post = text[e] if e < len(text) else None
    print(i, repr(prev), repr(post))

import json
print(json.dumps({"citations": entries}, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))

i = 0
s=entries[i]["span_start"]; e=entries[i]["span_end"]
text[s-1], text[s], text[e-1], text[e]
